title: Papaver leiocarpum
taxonomy: Papaver leiocarpum is classified within the plant family Papaveraceae, commonly known as the poppy family. This classification is part of the larger group of flowering plants known as angiosperms. Within the Papaveraceae family, Papaver leiocarpum is placed in the genus Papaver, which consists of species commonly referred to as poppies. The specific classification of Papaver leiocarpum is further divided into the sections and series within the Papaver genus. The exact classification within the Papaver genus may be subject to ongoing research and updates.
uses: Papaver leiocarpum is known for its medicinal properties, which have been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. benefits: Papaver leiocarpum, also known as the Himalayan poppy, has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine due to its potential health benefits. constituents: Papaver leiocarpum, also known as the Turkish poppy, is a plant that contains various active constituents, including alkaloids such as protopine and sanguinarine.
